title: Big Bang
body: The universe erupts into existence. The laws of thermodynamics that will one day govern Earth's climate are set in this first moment.

title: Earth forms from solar nebula
body: A spinning disk of gas and dust collapses under gravity. Our planet accretes — a molten, airless rock bombarded by asteroids and comets.

title: First oceans condense
body: As Earth cools, water vapour precipitates into the first global ocean. The hydrological cycle — the engine of climate — begins.

title: First life appears
body: Microbial life emerges in warm, shallow seas. Over the next billion years, biology will fundamentally reshape the atmosphere and climate.

title: Great Oxidation Event
body: Cyanobacteria flood the atmosphere with O₂. Anaerobic life faces mass extinction. The atmosphere shifts permanently toward the oxygen-rich air we breathe today.

title: Snowball Earth I
body: Ice sheets reach the equator. Global temperatures plummet to −40°C. Life retreats to hydrothermal vents. The planet is encased in ice for millions of years.

title: Permian extinction — 96% of life dies
body: Siberian mega-volcanoes pump CO₂ for a million years. Ocean temperatures rise 10°C. 96% of marine species go extinct in Earth's worst mass extinction.

title: Chicxulub asteroid — end of dinosaurs
body: A 12 km asteroid hits Mexico. Fires, then a decade-long impact winter kills 75% of all species. The climate engine resets.

title: PETM — oceans warm 8°C in 20,000 years
body: Carbon floods the atmosphere from the deep ocean. The closest geological twin of modern climate change — a case study in tipping points.

title: Pleistocene ice ages begin
body: Milankovitch cycles drive 100,000-year glacial pulses. Sea levels oscillate by 120m. The conditions that shaped human evolution.

title: Last Glacial Maximum — ice sheets peak
body: 3 km of ice covers Europe and North America. Sea level is 120m lower. CO₂ is 180 ppm. The world looks nothing like today.

title: Younger Dryas — sudden 1,200-year freeze
body: As the ice age ended, the Atlantic circulation collapsed. Temperatures in Europe plunged 15°C in a decade — a warning about tipping points.

title: Holocene begins — stable climate for civilisation
body: The climate enters an unusually warm, stable phase. Agriculture, cities, writing, and all of human history unfold within this narrow climatic window.

title: Little Ice Age begins
body: Alpine glaciers advance across Europe. The Thames freezes repeatedly. Agricultural failures trigger famines and conflict across the Northern Hemisphere.

title: Fourier: Earth's atmosphere traps heat
body: Joseph Fourier proposes the atmosphere acts like glass — the first description of the greenhouse effect. A foundational moment in climate science.

title: Eunice Foote: CO₂ traps solar heat
body: Foote proves CO₂ retains heat better than ordinary air — three years before Tyndall, and entirely overlooked for over a century.

title: Tyndall proves the infrared greenhouse
body: John Tyndall measures exactly which gases absorb infrared radiation. CO₂ and water vapour are the key. The physics of climate change is now established.

title: Arrhenius: CO₂ doubling warms Earth 5°C
body: After a year of hand calculations, Svante Arrhenius predicts CO₂ doubling raises global temperature by ~5°C — within the range modern models give.

title: Callendar: fossil fuels are warming Earth
body: G.S. Callendar shows 0.3°C of warming since the 1880s and directly attributes it to rising CO₂ from fossil fuels. The scientific community is largely unconvinced.

title: Great Smog of London — 12,000 dead
body: Five days of coal smog kill 12,000 Londoners. The disaster forces the first major clean air legislation, reshaping how governments think about atmospheric pollution.

title: Keeling Curve: CO₂ monitored at Mauna Loa
body: Charles Keeling begins measuring atmospheric CO₂ at Mauna Loa. The resulting curve — rising every year — becomes the most important graph in climate science.

title: First Earth Day — 20 million march
body: 20 million Americans fill the streets for the first Earth Day — the largest environmental demonstration in history. The EPA is created within months.

title: Antarctic ozone hole confirmed
body: British scientists confirm a catastrophic seasonal ozone hole over Antarctica, proving human pollution can destroy planetary-scale atmospheric systems.

title: Montreal Protocol — the ozone fix
body: 196 nations agree to phase out ozone-depleting chemicals — the most universally ratified treaty in UN history and the most successful environmental agreement ever.

title: IPCC established
body: The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change is founded to distil the world's climate science for governments. It will publish six comprehensive assessments over 35 years.

title: Hansen to Senate: global warming has begun
body: NASA's James Hansen testifies he is '99% confident' human warming has arrived. Delivered during a record heatwave, the testimony puts climate on the political map.

title: Pinatubo erupts — Earth cools 0.5°C
body: The largest eruption in 80 years injects 17 Mt of SO₂ into the stratosphere. Global temperatures fall 0.5°C — a natural experiment that validates climate models.

title: Rio Summit — UNFCCC signed
body: The Earth Summit produces the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change — the legal chassis for Kyoto, Paris, and every future climate agreement.

title: Kyoto Protocol — first binding targets
body: For the first time, rich nations accept legally binding greenhouse gas limits. Imperfect but historic — it establishes the precedent of binding international climate law.

title: European heatwave — 70,000 dead
body: France reaches 40°C. ~70,000 people die across Western Europe. Attribution science shows human warming made the event at least five times more likely.

title: Hurricane Katrina — 1,800 dead, $125B damage
body: Category 5 Katrina devastates New Orleans. Warming oceans are intensifying hurricane destructive potential. Climate intersects catastrophically with inequality.

title: IPCC 4th Report: warming is 'unequivocal'
body: The Fourth Assessment Report declares warming 'unequivocal'. The IPCC and Al Gore share the Nobel Peace Prize. The science is settled — the politics are not.

title: Arctic sea ice: record low — half gone
body: September sea ice falls to 3.41 million km² — 50% below the 1979 baseline. The Arctic is warming four times faster than the global average.

title: Paris Agreement — 196 nations, 1.5°C goal
body: COP21 in Paris produces a universal climate agreement. 196 parties commit to holding warming well below 2°C — the first time 1.5°C enters international law.

title: 2016: Hottest year on record — 1.1°C
body: 2016 shatters the temperature record for the third consecutive year. All 20 of the hottest years ever recorded fall in the 21st century.

title: Pacific Northwest heat dome — 49.6°C in Canada
body: A stationary high pushes Lytton, BC to 49.6°C. Over 1,400 people die. Attribution: 'virtually impossible' without human climate change.

title: Pakistan floods — one third of country submerged
body: Record monsoon rains submerge 33% of Pakistan, killing 1,700 and displacing 8 million. Climate change made the extreme rainfall 50% more likely.

title: 2023: Hottest year in 125,000 years
body: Every month of 2023 sets a global temperature record. The year averages 1.45°C above pre-industrial — almost certainly the warmest in 125,000 years.

title: CO₂ hits 426 ppm — highest in 3 million years
body: Atmospheric CO₂ crosses 426 ppm — the highest in at least 3 million years. The last time CO₂ was this high, sea levels were 20m higher.
